titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of BL Add. Ch. 66568

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=613 status=live modified=2018-11-28 15:17:26.344022+00 | translation moa=612 status=live modified=2018-11-28 15:14:42.386752+00

div.p:
Uniuersis Sancte matris ecclesie filiis . Svanus filius thorii salutem . Sciatis me dedisse et concessisse . et hac mea carta con
firmasse . Deo et Sancto Michaeli de Scone et canonicis ibidem deo seruientibus et seruituris  Ahednepobbel . per easdem diuisas cum
quibus Robertus capellanus illam tenuit . Et toftum unum in tubermure . illum scilicet quem aurifaber tenuit . Pratum quoque
quod est super Lochethin ex australi parte . a loco quo in oriente contiguum est terre rusticorum . quantumcumque extenditur per latum
et longum uersus occidentem cum communi pastura et aisiamento nemoris mei ad quecumque necesse habuerint . in liberam et perpetu
am elemosinam  pro anima comitis henrici . et omnium antecessorum necnon et successorum meorum . et pro anima mea . Quare uolo . ut prefati canonici prenominatam terram cum omnibus supradictis teneant et possideant iure perpetuo de me et heredibus meis mihi in per
petuum successuris . Ita libere et quiete . plenarie et honorifice  sicut aliqua domus religionis in toto Regno . ab aliquo baro
ne liberius tenet et quietius . plenarius et honorificentius . Hiis testibus . Iohanne episcopo Dunkeldensi . Roberto de berkelei . Hugone de kaled’ . Macbeth
iudice de gourin . Galfrido capellano de pert . Willelmo clerico de forfar . henrico capellano . Philippo vuieth . Iacob de pert Andr’. et Willelmo fratribus
eiusdem . Galfrido filio martini . Dauid et henrico filiis eius . Waltero de sancto eadmundo . et Multis Aliis ;

p.cl: To all sons of Holy Mother Church, Swain son of Thor [sends] greeting.

to have given, conceded, and by this my charter to have made firm, to God, to Saint Michael at Scone, and to the canons serving God in that place now and in the future, ‘Ahednepobbel’, by the same boundaries that Robert the chaplain held it; and one toft in Tibbermore, that is, the one that the goldsmith held. Also a meadow that is above the Lochty on the southern side, from the point where to the east it adjoins the land of the peasants, as far as it stretches through its breadth and length towards the west, with common pasture and easement of the wood for what things so ever they shall have need, in free and everlasting alms, for the soul of Earl Henry, and of all my ancestors as well as my successors, and for my own soul. Wherefore I will that the aforementioned canons hold and possess the aforenamed land with everything written above by everlasting right of me and my heirs who shall succeed me in perpetuity, so freely and quitly, fully, and honourably as any religious house in the whole kingdom most freely and quitely, fully and honourably, holds from any baron.

With these witnesses: John, bishop of Dunkeld; Robert of Berkeley; Hugh of Calder; Macbeth, judex of Gowrie; Geoffrey, chaplain of Perth; William, clerk of Forfar; Henry the chaplain; Philip Uvieth; James of Perth, Andrew and William, brothers of the same; Geoffrey fitz Martin, David and Henry his sons; Walter of St Edmunds; and many others.
